behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundary between watcher backend and pipeline intake (recursive watch behavior). REAL-INFRA: depends on real OS watcher semantics for subdirectories (e.g., recursive support, event paths), so System. MOCK-HIDE: a fake watcher that emits subdir events would pass even if the real backend never emits them. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss 'path filter accepts subdir files but OS watcher never reports subdir events'; Integration would miss 'in-process fake emits subdir events, but real backend on this OS/watch mode does not.' REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely filesystem watching/processing with no AI/ML call or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temporary directory tree, create `watched/sub/a.txt`, and assert one completed transform/output for that file path without restart. Use real filesystem events (no mocked watcher).

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets dedup algorithm for same path + time window; this can be validated at one object/function boundary with injected clock. REAL-INFRA: none required. MOCK-HIDE: not applicable if using deterministic clock and direct calls. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text says 'deduplication logic' for 'same file path within a configurable time window' and does not require OS events/network/subprocess or cross-module handoff to manifest.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test dedup component with fake clock: first request accepted, second identical path within window rejected, request after window accepted; include boundary at exact window edge.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: explicit behavior text references OS emitting multiple events; failure occurs at OS→watcher→dedup handoff. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem event behavior (duplicate/variant events) to reproduce. MOCK-HIDE: mocked single canonical event stream can hide duplicate-event patterns from real OS/backend. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss 'debounce logic works for identical event objects but fails on real rename+change pair'; Integration would miss 'fake watcher emits controlled duplicates, but real backend emits different event ordering/types causing double processing.' REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision path in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: On a real filesystem, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (create+append/atomic replace) and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output artifact for the target file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in watcher lifecycle across real directory inode removal/recreation while process remains alive. REAL-INFRA: requires real OS filesystem + watcher backend behavior for delete/recreate semantics. MOCK-HIDE: fake watcher can trivially 'rebind' and pass while real backend keeps stale watch handle. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss 'recovery code sets state to watching but underlying handle is dead after rm -rf'; Integration would miss 'simulated watcher reconnects, but real backend does not emit events for recreated path until full restart.' REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI/ML call in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering depends on interaction between arrival events, filesystem timestamps, and scheduler queueing. REAL-INFRA: real FS timestamp precision/semantics and event timing can change ordering behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory fixtures with idealized timestamps can hide same-tick or low-precision timestamp collisions seen on real FS. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss 'sort function is correct but actual metadata read (`birthtime` unavailable/rounded) causes wrong effective keys'; Integration would miss 'mocked metadata has unique ms precision, but real FS truncates leading to nondeterministic order bug.' REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior is file ordering logic with no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files near-simultaneously on a real filesystem with controlled creation times, run daemon, and assert processing order matches observed creation timestamps (including tie-handling expectation).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at boundary of real write-in-progress state vs watcher-trigger timing and pickup policy. REAL-INFRA: requires real concurrent file write behavior/events (partial writes, flush timing, rename/close). MOCK-HIDE: mocked 'file complete' flags hide races where daemon reads partial content from real disk. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss 'readiness check logic passes on static buffers but daemon reads truncated file during real streaming write'; Integration would miss 'fake FS reports stable size instantly, while real FS shows intermediate sizes causing premature processing.' REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is filesystem race handling only, no AI/ML stages.
behaviors.test_description: Write a large file in chunks with delays on real disk while daemon runs; verify processing starts only after final chunk/close and output reflects full final content once.
